summary: Crystal‑clear Betty soundboard captures a blistering 1978 show highlighted by a smoking Jack Straw, epic Scarlet > Fire and soaring Estimated Prophet/Eyes.
review: The recording is an exemplary Betty board – crisp, balanced and free of the usual tape hiss that plagues many archive releases.  Musically the band is on fire: Jerry’s solos sparkle from the opening Jack Straw through a monster Scarlet > Fire, while the first‑set flow stays tight and the second set delivers standout Estimated Prophet/Eyes, Peggy‑O and a joyous Iko Iko.  Overall the performance feels both relaxed and intensely focused, making it one of the most frequently recommended ‘78 dates.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 40 reviews (mostly 5‑star) consistently praise both the superb soundboard quality and the high‑energy, tight performance; only a handful of lower or neutral scores appear, so the combined show (70 %) + recording (30 %) rating lands in the top tier.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Repeated mentions of ‘crisp’, ‘superb Betty board’, ‘fantastic clarity’ and no reported drop‑outs; a few reviewers note the unusually long drum segment but it does not detract from overall fidelity.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers describe a tight, blazing first set that builds momentum into an equally powerful second set; transitions (Scarlet > Fire, Estimated > Eyes) are highlighted as seamless and the overall pacing is praised as “blazing” yet relaxed.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s tone and solos are repeatedly called ‘A++’, ‘explosive’, and ‘on fire’, especially on Jack Straw, Scarlet > Fire and the Estimated Prophet/Eyes sequence.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass is noted as the solid jazz foundation that lets the band roam; a few reviewers mention his prominent role in the Eyes jam.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir’s rhythm guitar and vocal delivery are praised on Minglewood Blues and other blues‑rock numbers, with occasional nods to his vocal phrasing.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith (and occasionally Brent) receive high marks – “on fire” on Mama and Minglewood, keyboards highlighted as a standout element throughout the set.
band_member_comments.Drums: The dual drummers are described as ‘solid’, ‘tight’, with an especially praised Drum segment that adds energy without becoming sloppy.
